attacking_style: Launches late-race solo attacks from established breakaways at approximately 25km to go, targeting intermediate sprints or final acceleration points. Prefers attacking from already-formed breaks rather than initiating from peloton.
team_tactics: Insufficient data to establish clear team support patterns. Appears to operate with autonomy once in breakaway situations.
terrain_preference: Insufficient data to determine specific terrain preferences, though breakaway participation suggests suitability for transitional or reduced-bunch sprint stages.
vulnerability: Attacks lack sufficient power differential when peloton maintains controlled tempo (sub-40kph). Solo efforts from breaks fail to generate sustainable gaps beyond 1 minute against organized chase.
key_patterns: Attacks from breakaway at exactly 25km to go in multiple races (2 observations).
Solo accelerations from established breaks rather than peloton-initiated moves (2 observations).
Unable to create winning margins against controlled peloton speeds, gaps limited to 1 minute (2 observations).
form_trajectory: neutral: Consistent tactical approach but ineffective execution, suggesting stable but insufficient form to convert breakaway attacks into results
